title: Oscar's First Day Garden Adventure
synopsis: Oscar feels scared on his first day at a new preschool, but discovers a magical garden where he makes friends with other children and learns that new places can be wonderful when you're brave enough to explore them.

pages.text: Oscar stood outside his new preschool with his mama. His tummy felt funny and his hands were shaky. "I'm scared," he whispered, holding mama's hand tight. The big building looked so different from his old home.

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with curly black hair wearing a bright blue backpack stands nervously next to his mother outside a cheerful yellow preschool building. Oscar's brown eyes look worried as he clutches his mother's hand. The school has colorful windows and a welcoming entrance with flowers. The scene is drawn in bright cartoon style with soft, textured backgrounds resembling construction paper.
illustration.alt: Oscar, a nervous young Latino boy, holds his mother's hand outside his new preschool

pages.text: "It's okay to feel scared," mama said with a warm smile. "New places can feel big and scary at first. But maybe you'll find something wonderful inside." She gave Oscar a gentle hug. "Remember, you're very brave."

illustration.description: Oscar's mother, a kind woman with long dark hair in a colorful dress, kneels down to Oscar's level and gives him an encouraging hug. Her expression is warm and reassuring. Oscar still looks uncertain but is beginning to feel a little better. The background shows the school entrance with other diverse families arriving, creating a welcoming community atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Oscar's mother hugs him reassuringly outside the preschool

pages.text: Inside the classroom, Oscar felt even more scared. There were so many new faces! His teacher, Ms. Chen, had a kind smile. "Would you like to see our special garden, Oscar?" she asked. Oscar nodded quietly, curious but still worried.

illustration.description: A bright, cheerful classroom with diverse children playing at different stations. Ms. Chen, an Asian teacher with short black hair wearing a green apron, smiles warmly at Oscar. Some children are reading, others are building with blocks. The walls are decorated with colorful artwork and plants. Oscar stands close to the door, still looking hesitant but interested.
illustration.alt: Oscar meets his teacher Ms. Chen in a busy, colorful preschool classroom

pages.text: The garden was amazing! Tall sunflowers reached toward the sky like friendly giants. Red tomatoes hung like tiny balloons. Oscar's eyes grew wide with wonder. "Wow!" he whispered, forgetting to be scared for just a moment.

illustration.description: A magical school garden with towering yellow sunflowers, bright red tomato plants, and lush green vegetables in neat rows. Oscar stands in the middle of the garden path, his mouth open in amazement and his arms slightly outstretched. Butterflies flutter around colorful flowers, and the sun shines brightly overhead. The garden feels alive and welcoming with rich, vibrant colors.
illustration.alt: Oscar discovers a wonderful school garden full of sunflowers and vegetables

pages.text: "Hi! I'm Maya!" said a girl with pigtails, holding a small watering can. "And I'm Jamal!" called a boy picking cherry tomatoes. They both looked happy and friendly. Oscar felt a little flutter of excitement instead of fear.

illustration.description: Maya, a Black girl with neat pigtails wearing a pink shirt, waves at Oscar while holding a small blue watering can. Jamal, a boy with medium brown skin and short hair in an orange t-shirt, smiles broadly while reaching for red cherry tomatoes. Both children have warm, welcoming expressions. Oscar looks surprised but pleased to meet them.
illustration.alt: Maya and Jamal, two friendly children, welcome Oscar to the school garden

pages.text: "Want to help us water the plants?" Maya asked. Oscar nodded, feeling braver now. As he helped pour water on the thirsty flowers, he started to smile. Maybe this new place wasn't so scary after all! He felt proud and happy.

illustration.description: All three children work together in the garden. Oscar carefully holds a small green watering can, his face showing concentration and growing confidence. Maya and Jamal work alongside him, all three smiling as water sparkles over the colorful flowers. The garden around them blooms with life, and Oscar's posture is more relaxed and happy. Dewdrops glisten on leaves in the warm sunlight.
illustration.alt: Oscar, Maya, and Jamal work together happily watering plants in the school garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Oscar feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Oscar felt scared when he first arrived at his new preschool. It's normal to feel scared in new places!

questions.question: What did Ms. Chen show Oscar?
questions.explanation: Ms. Chen showed Oscar the special school garden with sunflowers and vegetables.

questions.question: Who were the two children Oscar met in the garden?
questions.explanation: Oscar met Maya, who had a watering can, and Jamal, who was picking tomatoes.

questions.question: How did Oscar feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: By the end, Oscar felt happy and brave because he made new friends and discovered the wonderful garden.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Oscar felt _____ at his new school, but then he found a beautiful _____ with tall sunflowers!

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Maya and Jamal were _____ to Oscar. They helped him water the _____ and Oscar felt much _____!
